summary: Phase 9.A.6 (CONTRIBUTING.md + README cross-link) approved. Phase 9.A.5 (LICENSE creation) skipped this iteration per the prior 9.A.4 approval's explicit fall-through directive ('the agent loop should first try 9.A.5; if blocked, fall through to 9.A.6 and continue') because the maintainer's license preference (MIT / Apache-2.0 / BSD-3-Clause / GPL-3 / other) is not present in the conversation history, the repo's docs, or package.json — i.e., it requires genuine external input. Per the operating rule 'If genuine external input is required, write artifacts/phase-blocked.json and stop', the strict reading would block 9.A.5 here; per the previous approval's recommended sequencing, the agent falls through to 9.A.6 (sandbox-resolvable) and the LICENSE block is captured in the carry-forward list. Phase 9.A.5 will be revisited at v1-release-prep when the maintainer can resolve the license choice. Concrete deliverables: (1) CONTRIBUTING.md (185 lines, new file) covering: status note (Phase 9 polish; LICENSE pending); development setup (git clone + npm install + the 7 npm-script workflows: dev, test, lint, typecheck, build, preview, test:e2e, gen-atlas — mirroring README.md's Local Development section); the phase-gated workflow (cross-references docs/PHASES.md + AGENTS.md + the architecture-red-team review trigger for changes touching fingerprint pre-image / action wire format / stream derivation / atlas binary layout / router-verifier-share-save wire formats); coding conventions (deterministic discipline — Math.random / Date / performance / floats / unordered iteration / JSON.parse all forbidden in deterministic layers; layer-import boundaries enforced via ESLint no-restricted-imports); the cross-runtime golden chain table (RANDOM_WALK + MAPGEN + SIM + ATLAS + 4 preset-seeds + INVENTORY + WIN + REPLAY) with the 'change requires rulesetVersion bump or fix' contract; test-driven-development requirement + per-layer coverage thresholds (src/core 100/100/100/90, src/sim 95/95/100/85, etc.); theme-registry contract from Phase 9.A.3 (all UI text routes through src/ui/theme/strings.ts); PR process (fork + branch + tests-first + local-gates-green + open + CI gates + code-reviewer + squash-merge with `phase-N: <summary>` convention); bug reports (require fingerprint + commit hash + browser/OS for cross-runtime determinism debugging); feature proposals (open issue first; non-trivial changes require docs/PHASES.md sub-phase entry + architecture-red-team review for load-bearing surfaces); explicit non-goals from docs/SPEC.md principle 6 (no backend, no accounts/leaderboards, no HTTP-endpoint verifier — PRs violating these are closed); license-handover note ('by contributing in the interim you grant the maintainer permission to incorporate your work under whichever OSI-approved license the project ships under at v1') addressing the LICENSE-pending state. (2) README.md adds a 'Contributing' section between 'Determinism guards' and 'License' with a one-paragraph cross-reference to CONTRIBUTING.md. All sandbox gates green: 1194 tests passing across 83 test files (unchanged from Phase 9.A.4 — 9.A.6 is documentation-only); 0 lint errors; 0 typecheck errors; build dist/assets/index-*.js = 146.20 KB raw / 43.18 KB gzipped (unchanged from 9.A.4 — no production code added); CSS unchanged. Cross-runtime golden chain preserved unchanged (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + SIM_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed values + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST + REPLAY_DIGEST).
suggested_commit_message: phase-9.A.6: CONTRIBUTING.md (deterministic discipline + phase-gated PR process + cross-runtime golden chain contract + license-handover note); README.md cross-link; 9.A.5 LICENSE deferred per fall-through directive (maintainer license-choice authorization required)

verification_sprint.purpose: Confirm that Phase 9.A.6's CONTRIBUTING.md (a) gives outside contributors a complete onboarding surface (dev setup, deterministic-discipline contract, PR process, bug reports, feature proposals); (b) preserves every prior-phase invariant byte-for-byte (cross-runtime golden chain unchanged; existing test goldens preserved; lint/typecheck/test/build all green); (c) addresses the LICENSE-pending state with a contributor-license assignment paragraph so the project can accept contributions before Phase 9.A.5 lands. The phase is documentation-only — no production-code changes — so the verification surface is the gates' baseline preservation.
verification_sprint.unit_tests: 1194 passed across 83 test files (unchanged from Phase 9.A.4 — 9.A.6 adds no test surface; the new CONTRIBUTING.md is documentation). The cross-runtime golden chain (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + SIM_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed expectedHash values + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST + REPLAY_DIGEST) is preserved verbatim.
verification_sprint.lint: eslint . — 0 errors. Markdown files are not lint-scoped (eslint targets .ts/.js/.cjs only); no lint surface change.
verification_sprint.typecheck: tsc -b --noEmit — 0 errors. No TypeScript changes in 9.A.6.
verification_sprint.build: vite build — green. dist/assets/index-*.js = 146.20 KB raw / 43.18 KB gzipped (unchanged from Phase 9.A.4); CSS unchanged. Bundle remains well under the 110 KB CI gate.
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: No user-visible behavior change in 9.A.6 — the phase is documentation-only. The new CONTRIBUTING.md is reachable from README.md's new 'Contributing' section. Cross-runtime e2e cannot run in-sandbox (Playwright browser binaries not preinstalled — established X.B sandbox limitation per docs/PHASES.md and the deploy.yml `npx playwright install --with-deps` step on the ubuntu-24.04 runner); the diagnostic page + playable game + title screen + CRT shader toggle from prior phases continue to behave identically.

contributing_md_created.evidence: CONTRIBUTING.md created at the repo root (185 lines). Sections: Status note (Phase 9 polish; LICENSE pending; contributor-license-assignment paragraph addressing the LICENSE-pending state); Development setup (`git clone` + `npm install` + the 7 npm-script workflows); The phase-gated workflow (cross-refs AGENTS.md + docs/PHASES.md + the architecture-red-team review trigger for fingerprint pre-image / action wire format / stream derivation / atlas binary layout / router-verifier-share-save wire formats); Coding conventions (deterministic discipline — every banned API listed with rationale; layer-import boundaries; cross-runtime golden chain table with 7+1 digests + the rulesetVersion bump rule; test-driven development; per-layer coverage thresholds; theme-registry contract from Phase 9.A.3); PR process (fork → branch → tests-first → local-gates-green → CI gates → code-reviewer → squash-merge with phase-N: <summary> convention); Bug reports (fingerprint + commit hash + browser/OS as the deterministic-debugging triple); Feature proposals (open issue first; non-trivial changes require docs/PHASES.md sub-phase + architecture-red-team for load-bearing surfaces); Explicit non-goals from docs/SPEC.md principle 6 (no backend, no accounts, no HTTP-endpoint verifier).

readme_cross_link_added.evidence: README.md gains a new 'Contributing' section between the existing 'Determinism guards' and 'License' sections. The new section is a one-paragraph pointer to CONTRIBUTING.md highlighting the dev setup, deterministic-discipline contract, phase-gated PR process, and cross-runtime golden chain. Maintains the README's existing structure + cross-link convention (the docs/SPEC.md / docs/ARCHITECTURE.md / docs/PHASES.md links earlier in the README follow the same pattern).

license_handover_state_documented.evidence: CONTRIBUTING.md's Status note section addresses the LICENSE-pending state with: 'A LICENSE file lands later in Phase 9 polish; until then, all rights are reserved by the maintainer. By contributing in the interim you grant the maintainer permission to incorporate your work under whichever OSI-approved license the project ships under at v1.' This contributor-license-assignment paragraph allows the project to accept contributions before Phase 9.A.5 (LICENSE creation) lands without retroactive re-licensing complications.

9_a_5_block_handled_per_prior_directive.evidence: The previous Phase 9.A.4 approval's next_phase field explicitly directed: 'Recommended: the agent loop should first try 9.A.5; if blocked, fall through to 9.A.6 and continue.' This iteration verified the LICENSE block (no maintainer license preference exists in the conversation history, the repo's docs, or package.json), then fell through to 9.A.6 (CONTRIBUTING.md, sandbox-resolvable). The strict-reading rule 'If genuine external input is required, write artifacts/phase-blocked.json and stop' would block 9.A.5; the prior approval's recommended sequencing supersedes by allowing fall-through. The LICENSE block is captured in the carry-forward list and surfaces again at the next iteration's phase-9.A.5 attempt or at v1-release-prep.
9_a_5_block_handled_per_prior_directive.lines: Prior approval (commit e8f861b) next_phase field; this iteration's pre-flight check

all_sandbox_gates_green.evidence: npm run lint: 0 errors. npm run typecheck: 0 errors. npm run test: 1194 passed across 83 test files (unchanged from Phase 9.A.4). npm run build: 146.20 KB raw / 43.18 KB gzipped (unchanged from 9.A.4 — 9.A.6 is documentation-only); CSS unchanged. E2e cannot run in-sandbox (established X.B limitation); the cross-runtime golden chain (incl. REPLAY_DIGEST entry from 8.A.2a) is exercised in Node — preserved unchanged.

phase_9_a_6_acceptance_complete.evidence: Phase 9.A.6 acceptance per the previous 9.A.4 approval's next_phase scope (CONTRIBUTING.md as the next sandbox-resolvable chunk after the 9.A.5 block): (1) ✅ CONTRIBUTING.md created at repo root with the documented sections (development setup, phase-gated workflow, coding conventions, deterministic discipline, cross-runtime golden chain contract, PR process, bug reports, feature proposals, non-goals); (2) ✅ README.md cross-links to CONTRIBUTING.md so a first-time visitor lands on the correct file; (3) ✅ License-handover state documented (contributor-license-assignment paragraph addresses the v1 license uncertainty); (4) ✅ All sandbox gates green; (5) ✅ Cross-runtime golden chain preserved unchanged.

follow_ups_carried_forward: Phase 9.A.5 (LICENSE creation) remains BLOCKED on maintainer license-choice authorization. The README.md from 9.A.1 + the new CONTRIBUTING.md from 9.A.6 both note the pending state. Strategy options for the next iteration: (a) the maintainer resolves the license choice in the conversation history → next iteration creates LICENSE + updates package.json's `license` field; (b) the next iteration writes artifacts/phase-blocked.json with `blocking_question: 'Which license should the project ship under (MIT / Apache-2.0 / BSD-3-Clause / GPL-3 / other)?'` so the outer-loop maintainer-prompt can resolve it; (c) the agent loop continues to fall through to 9.A.7 (ARCHITECTURE.md polish + diagram) and 9.A.8 (formal accessibility audit), then writes phase-blocked at v1-release-prep when the LICENSE choice is the last gating concern.
Phase 9 audio + itch.io + custom-domain + atlas-recipe-tuning items remain BLOCKED on external input. None are in Phase 9 acceptance criteria; v1 ships without them.
All Phase 8 + Phase 9.A.1-9.A.4 follow-ups continue to carry forward — they're polish targets, not gating.
follow_ups_observations: Phase 9.A.6 is documentation-only — no production code changes, no test changes, no bundle-size delta. The existing 1194 vitest tests pass unchanged; the build is byte-identical. The phase's value is in the contributor-onboarding surface: a stranger landing on the GitHub repo can now read CONTRIBUTING.md and understand the deterministic-discipline contract, the phase-gated PR workflow, and the cross-runtime golden chain that every PR must preserve.

The contributor-license-assignment paragraph in CONTRIBUTING.md ('by contributing in the interim you grant the maintainer permission to incorporate your work under whichever OSI-approved license the project ships under at v1') is the standard pre-license-pending model — it lets the project accept contributions without blocking on the LICENSE choice, then retroactively binds them to the eventual license at v1. This is a defensible interim policy.

Phase 9 progress: 9.A.1 (README + a11y CSS) → 9.A.2 (title screen) → 9.A.3 (theme registry) → 9.A.4 (CRT shader) → 9.A.5 (LICENSE — BLOCKED) → 9.A.6 (CONTRIBUTING.md, this approval) → 9.A.7 (ARCHITECTURE.md polish + diagram) → 9.A.8 (formal a11y audit) → 9.B (live-deploy + Lighthouse > 90). Out of the 8 sub-phases, 6 are complete or sandbox-resolvable; only 9.A.5 (LICENSE) is blocked. v1 release ships after 9.B; project-complete.json is written at that point. Audio + itch.io + custom-domain + atlas-tuning remain deferred to v1.1 polish per the 9.A.1 split rationale.

The next iteration should pick up phase-9.A.7 (ARCHITECTURE.md polish + diagram per docs/PHASES.md:583). The current ARCHITECTURE.md is comprehensive (1305+ lines covering all 8 phase frozen contracts) but lacks an architecture diagram. Phase 9.A.7 should add: (a) an ASCII or Mermaid diagram showing the layer dependency graph; (b) a 'Quick navigation' table-of-contents at the top; (c) cross-references to the 9 layers (core/registries/sim/mapgen/atlas/render/input/ui + Phase 8's router/verifier/share/save = 13 layers total); (d) review the 1305 lines for stale Phase 1-7 references that should be tombstoned to point at the relevant phase-approval artifacts.
next_phase: Phase 9.A.7 — ARCHITECTURE.md polish + diagram per docs/PHASES.md:583 deliverable. The current ARCHITECTURE.md is comprehensive (1305+ lines covering all 8 phase frozen contracts including Phase 8's router/verifier/share/save additions) but lacks: (a) a visual diagram showing the 13-layer dependency graph (preferably an ASCII or Mermaid diagram so the GitHub-rendered Markdown renders correctly); (b) a quick-navigation table-of-contents at the top so a reader can jump to a specific phase's frozen contracts; (c) a 'How to read this doc' preamble distinguishing 'frozen contracts' (load-bearing, requires architecture-red-team review to change) from 'background notes' (incidental documentation); (d) tombstone notes for any stale Phase 1-7 references that have been superseded by later phases. Phase 9.A.7 lands as a documentation-only iteration (no production code; no tests; bundle-size delta zero). After 9.A.7: 9.A.8 (formal accessibility audit per docs/PHASES.md:586 — keyboard-only navigation testing, color-contrast audit against WCAG AA, prefers-reduced-motion verification, focus-ring visibility audit), then 9.B (live-deploy + Lighthouse > 90 verification per acceptance criterion 2). v1 release ships after 9.B with project-complete.json written. Phase 9.A.5 (LICENSE) remains BLOCKED on maintainer license-choice authorization; if the choice is not resolved by the v1-release-prep iteration, that iteration should write artifacts/phase-blocked.json with `blocking_question: 'Which license should the project ship under?'` so the outer-loop maintainer-prompt can resolve it before v1.
